name: Blueberry Polenta
recipeIngredient: 1 cup corn flour (maybe fine ground corn meal, at your own risk)
1/2 cup whole wheat flour (might add wheat germ to increase fiber)
2 tbsp honey (sorry, I think I've seen honey used before on this list)
2 tbsp Succanat (or 3 tbsp Succanat and no honey for vegans)
1/2 cup water (or use nonfat milk, juice, or soy moo)
1 1/2 tsp. Ener-g egg replacer (or 1 egg white)
2 tbsp nonfat yogurt (this is needed to make the baking soda work)
1/2 tsp. cinnamon (or as much as you can handle, ie. more...)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mash bananas with a potato masher.
2. In a large bowl, add all remaining ingredients except for the blueberries and stir well.
3. Lightly oil a small pan or an 8-cup muffin tin by dipping a paper napkin in a drop of oil and spreading it evenly over the baking surface (or spray with Pam).
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan or muffin tin.
5. Sprinkle blueberries on top, adding as many as desired.
6. Bake at about 400°F for 25-35 minutes, until golden brown and set.
7. Serve warm, enjoying the rich flavor of melted blueberries.
